rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Use assign statements in implementation annexes only for existing output features or auxiliary variables
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation annexes
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers when available to maintain traceability
Avoid encoding AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed
Align helper declaration types with expression types to prevent type mismatches
Use concrete bounds or named constants for assumptions and guarantees
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks
Use eq for equations, const for constants, assume for assumptions, and guarantee for guarantees
Use assign only in implementation annexes for output features or auxiliary variables
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Avoid placing assumptions and guarantees in implementation annexes unless they are implementation-specific
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit exposure
